note: Ground truth is committed before any paired run and kept outside every standalone fixture copy presented to the auditor.

corrections.trigger: The first blinded control observation cited the limiter registration line and identified an unintended missing token-consumption control.
corrections.change: Accept either side of the mount-order evidence, record the second real violation, exclude case-01 from lift, and add pre-run replacement case-07.
corrections.reason: Calling the valid limiter citation a miss or the missing single-use transition a false positive would make the grader wrong. Because the correction followed a model observation, case-01 cannot support causal lift.

cases.claim: Excluded diagnostic fixture with two real violations discovered during the first control observation.

findings.claim: The password-reset route is registered before the limiter that is intended to protect password routes.
Successful reset-token verification does not mark the token used, so it remains reusable until expiration.

cases.claim: A reset-token digest is compared with ordinary string equality instead of a constant-time comparison.
Passwords are stored with one fast SHA-256 digest rather than a supported password KDF.
Reset tokens are generated with Math.random rather than a cryptographically secure generator.
A development environment branch bypasses password verification.
The control uses bcrypt, constant-time reset-token comparison, CSPRNG single-use tokens, generic errors, and limiter-before-route mounting.
The password-reset route is registered before the limiter that is intended to protect password routes; all other A-SEC-6 controls are present.
